articles.title: Why New Zealanders Are Moving To Australia in Record Numbers
articles.summary: About 41,000 New Zealanders moved to Australia in 2025, the most in 12 years, drawn by a more robust jobs market. When even former Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern left, it made international headlines. Net citizen migration is at its lowest since records began, and unlike in past waves, people aren't coming home. Former PM John Key, Cambridge Provost Gillian Tett, and a young lawyer who says he doubled his salary by leaving explain what's gone wrong and what it would take to reverse it. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Awaits Iranian Response After Hormuz Clashes Strain Ceasefire

Bloomberg News Deputy UAE Bureau Chief Abeer Abu Omar and White House Correspondent Jeff Mason join David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. Meanwhile, the Israeli military said an explosive drone launched from Lebanon was found near the Lebanese border in northern Israel. Hezbollah later claimed responsibility for the attack.

articles.title: Israeli settlers force Palestinian family to exhume father’s body in West Bank
articles.summary: Israeli settlers force Palestinian family to exhume father’s body in West Bank Israeli settlers forced a Palestinian family on Friday to exhume their father's body from a cemetery in the occupied West Bank. The settlers said the grave was too close to the recently re-established settlement of Sa-Nur, near Jenin, located around 300m from the burial site. This was despite the family of Hussein Asasa reportedly obtaining the necessary Israeli permits to bury him there. articles.title: EU chief urges increased humanitarian access in south Lebanon
articles.summary: EU chief urges increased humanitarian access in south Lebanon European Union crisis management chief Hadja Lahbib called for increased humanitarian access on Saturday, as aid struggles to reach areas in southern Lebanon hit by Israeli strikes. “Humanitarian aid is ready, but too often it cannot reach those who need it most,” Lahbib told a news conference ahead of an expected EU aid delivery. Officials and members of the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon stand next to the humanitarian aid being distributed in a village in southern Lebanon on 9 May 2026 (Kawnat Haju/AFP)

articles.title: ‘It’s Not Because They Want to Be Friends’: Brilliant on What to Expect From the Trump-Xi Summit

articles.title: Israel kills five in Gaza in past day, raising death toll to 72,736
articles.summary: Israel kills five in Gaza in past day, raising death toll to 72,736 Israeli forces killed at least five Palestinians and wounded 15 others over the past day, according to medical sources. This brings the death toll from Israel’s genocidal war on Gaza to 72,736, with 172,535 wounded since October 2023. Medical teams said many victims remain trapped under the rubble, as rescue crews are still unable to reach them.
